questions.stem: Under ASC 321, how should an equity investment without a readily determinable fair value be measured if the entity does not elect the fair value option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At cost less impairment, plus or minus observable price changes (measurement alternative)

choices.label: B
choices.text: At lower of cost or market

questions.explanation: ASC 321-10-35-2 provides a measurement alternative for equity securities without readily determinable fair values. Under this alternative, the investment is measured at cost, minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer.

questions.stem: Investor Corp owns 30% of Investee Corp and uses the equity method. Investee reported net income of $200,000 and paid dividends of $50,000. What is the effect on Investor's income?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Investment income of $60,000 and dividend income of $15,000

questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323-10-35-4), the investor recognizes its share of the investee's net income: 30% × $200,000 = $60,000 as investment income. Dividends received ($50,000 × 30% = $15,000) reduce the investment account but are not recognized as income. They represent a return of capital under the equity method.

questions.stem: Under ASC 320, unrealized gains and losses on available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities are reported in:
questions.explanation: ASC 320-10-35-1 requires AFS debt securities to be measured at fair value, with unrealized gains and losses excluded from earnings and reported in other comprehensive income (accumulated OCI on the balance sheet). They are reclassified to earnings when the securities are sold or an impairment is recognized.

questions.stem: Under ASC 321, unrealized gains and losses on equity securities with readily determinable fair values are reported in:
questions.explanation: ASU 2016-01 (codified in ASC 321-10-35-1) requires equity securities with readily determinable fair values to be measured at fair value with changes recognized in net income. This eliminated the previous available-for-sale category for equity securities, which had allowed unrealized gains/losses in OCI.

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-30, how is impairment of an available-for-sale debt security recognized when the entity intends to sell the security?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write down to fair value through OCI only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Write down the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value through earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize only the credit loss portion in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: No impairment recognition is required until actual sale

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-4, if the entity intends to sell (or more likely than not will be required to sell) the AFS debt security before recovery, the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value is recognized in earnings. If the entity does not intend to sell, only the credit loss portion is recognized in earnings (through an allowance), with the remainder in OCI.

questions.stem: Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported on the balance sheet at:
questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-25-1, held-to-maturity (HTM) securities are reported at amortized cost. The entity must have the positive intent and ability to hold the security to maturity. Any premium or discount is amortized over the life of the security using the effective interest method. Under CECL, HTM securities are also subject to the allowance for credit losses model.

questions.stem: Investor A acquired 25% of Company B for $500,000. The book value of Company B's net assets is $1,600,000. The excess of cost over underlying book value is attributed to equipment (fair value exceeds book value by $200,000, 10-year remaining life). What is Investor A's equity method income if Company B reports net income of $300,000?
questions.explanation: Share of net income: 25% × $300,000 = $75,000. Excess cost allocated to equipment: 25% × $200,000 = $50,000, amortized over 10 years = $5,000/year reduction. Equity method income: $75,000 - $5,000 = $70,000. The excess cost is amortized as an adjustment to equity method earnings.

questions.stem: When a debt security is transferred from held-to-maturity to available-for-sale, the security is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recorded at amortized cost with no adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recorded at the lower of amortized cost or fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10, when a HTM security is transferred to AFS, the security is reclassified at fair value. The unrealized holding gain or loss at the date of transfer is reported in OCI. Note that such transfers may call into question the entity's intent regarding remaining HTM securities.

questions.stem: Under ASC 825 (Fair Value Option), an entity elects to measure a financial liability at fair value. Where are subsequent changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk reported?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-45-5 (as amended by ASU 2016-01), for financial liabilities measured under the fair value option, changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk are presented separately in OCI. All other changes in fair value are recognized in net income.

questions.stem: Under ASC 810, which of the following generally requires consolidation of an investee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ownership of 20% or more of voting interests

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ownership of more than 50% of voting interests (controlling financial interest)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Any equity investment in the investee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, a parent with a controlling financial interest (usually more than 50% of voting interests) in a subsidiary must consolidate it. Ownership of 20-50% typically results in the equity method (significant influence), while less than 20% results in the fair value measurement under ASC 321.
